steps.title: Identify parabola's vertex and direction
steps.content:
The equation $ y = x^2 + 1.7 $ is a parabola in vertex form $ y = a(x-h)^2 + k $. Comparing directly, vertex is (0, 1.7) and opens upward (since coefficient of $ x^2 $ is positive).
Only choices A and B have a parabola with vertex at (0, 1.7).

steps.title: Determine line slope and eliminate choices
steps.content: The line $ y = 1.7 - x $ has slope -1 (negative), so it must slant sharply downward from left to right. Choices B and D show lines with positive slopes, which are invalid. Eliminate B and D.

steps.title: Validate vertex consistency
steps.content: Choice C claims the parabola's vertex is at (-1.7, 0). This conflicts with the equation's vertex at (0, 1.7). Eliminate C. Only A has both matching parabola vertex and downward-sloping line.
